title: Reports CSV export is vulnerable to spreadsheet formula injection
reasoning: The reports export quotes CSV fields but does not neutralize values beginning with =, +, -, or @. Several exported values come from backend data such as telecaller names, lead sources, and statuses. If an attacker or compromised data source stores a spreadsheet formula in one of those fields, opening the exported CSV in Excel or similar tools can execute the formula in the user's spreadsheet context.
reproduction: Create or mock a lead source named `=HYPERLINK("http://attacker.example","click")`, open Reports, export CSV, then open it in a spreadsheet application. The value is emitted as a formula-capable cell rather than neutralized text.
recommendation: Use the same formula-neutralizing escaping strategy consistently for all CSV exports, prefixing dangerous leading characters before quoting or otherwise forcing text cells. Apply it to Reports.jsx handleExport.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked, and there is no regression coverage asserting exported CSV neutralizes formula-leading user-controlled values.
suggestedRegressionTest: Unit test the reports CSV serialization with values starting with `=`, `+`, `-`, and `@`, asserting the generated cells are prefixed/escaped as text and still quote embedded quotes correctly.

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists in current src/pages/Reports.jsx: handleExport is at line 136, user/backend-controlled fields are exported at lines 147, 150, and 153, and CSV serialization at line 155 still only wraps fields in quotes and doubles embedded quotes. It does not prefix or otherwise neutralize cells beginning with =, +, -, or @. Other exports in Dashboard.jsx, LeadBox.jsx, and src/routes/reports.js contain formula-injection mitigations, which further confirms Reports.jsx has not received the same fix. No test/spec files were found and package.json has no test script; npm run lint was attempted but rejected by the read-only/no-approval command policy, so current-code inspection is the available evidence.
